title: Shirui National Park
tagline: Shirui National Park — wildlife reserve of Manipur

overview.short: Shirui National Park is a national park located in the state of Manipur in India.
overview.description: Shirui National Park is a national park located in the state of Manipur in India. It was established in 1982. Among the animals that make their homes here include tragopan, tiger and leopard. It is here that the famous shirui lily (Lilium mackliniae) grows naturally.

topPlaces.name: Moreh, India
topPlaces.description: Moreh (Meitei pronunciation: /mō-rey/) is a border town located on the India–Myanmar border in Tengnoupal district of the Indian state of Manipur at the entrance of the Kabaw Valley. As a rapidly developing international trade point with the integrated customs and international immigration checkpoint, Moreh plays an important role in India's Look East Policy, trade and commerce under ASEAN–India Free Trade Area, India-Myanmar relationship, India–Myanmar–Thailand road connectivity, and Trans-Asian Railway connectivity.

topPlaces.name: Tamu Township
topPlaces.description: Tamu Township is a township in Tamu District in the Sagaing Division of Burma. The principal town is Tamu.

topPlaces.name: Tamu, Myanmar
topPlaces.description: Tamu is a town in the Kabaw Valley in the Sagaing Region in north-west Myanmar near the border with the eastern Indian state of Manipur. It is a principal town of the Kabaw Valley and the seat of administration for the Tamu Township.

topPlaces.name: Yangoupokpi-Lokchao Wildlife Sanctuary
topPlaces.description: Yangoupokpi-Lokchao Wildlife Sanctuary (Meitei: Yangoupokpi-Lokchao Lamlak-ki Saa-Ngaa Ngaak Senpham) is an Indo-Burma wildlife sanctuary in Chandel district of Manipur. It is in the Indo-Myanmar border about 110 km from Imphal.

items.note: Moreh (Meitei pronunciation: /mō-rey/) is a border town located on the India–Myanmar border in Tengnoupal district of the Indian state of Manipur at the entrance of the Kabaw Valley. As a rapidly developing international trade point with the integrated customs and international immigration checkpoint, Moreh plays an important role in India's Look East Policy, trade and commerce under ASEAN–India Free Trade Area, India-Myanmar relationship, India–Myanmar–Thailand road connectivity, and Trans-Asian Railway connectivity.
Tamu Township is a township in Tamu District in the Sagaing Division of Burma. The principal town is Tamu.

Tamu is a town in the Kabaw Valley in the Sagaing Region in north-west Myanmar near the border with the eastern Indian state of Manipur. It is a principal town of the Kabaw Valley and the seat of administration for the Tamu Township.

Yangoupokpi-Lokchao Wildlife Sanctuary (Meitei: Yangoupokpi-Lokchao Lamlak-ki Saa-Ngaa Ngaak Senpham) is an Indo-Burma wildlife sanctuary in Chandel district of Manipur. It is in the Indo-Myanmar border about 110 km from Imphal.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Shirui National Park?
faq.a: The best time to visit Shirui National Park is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Shirui National Park?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Imphal Airport (68 km) and the nearest railway station is Dimapur Railway Station (193 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Shirui National Park?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Shirui National Park at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Shirui National Park?
faq.a: Stays in Shirui National Park start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Shirui National Park?
faq.a: The top attractions are Moreh, India, Tamu Township, Tamu, Myanmar, Yangoupokpi-Lokchao Wildlife Sanctuary.
